data.body:
# gerrit-verify wall-clock reduction — research, investigation, experiments, findings, plan

Ticket trail: investigation task crushable-choosable-llama (7654); epic
baseborn-subtle-cricket (ce0e) with children 8d36 (xdist), bad4 (pip cache),
9ad9 (--durations). Epic plan-reviewed → PASS, certified.

## Goal
Reduce gerrit-verify (Gerrit `Verified` gate) wall clock WITHOUT reducing assurance that a
change won't break main. Measure per-test perf; find what drives CI wall clock; run local
experiments to derisk changes; propose a proven plan. OSS research authorized.

## Environment / method
- Repo checkout, local .venv (Python 3.14.6; CI uses 3.11–3.13), 18 cores (CI runners
  ~4 vCPU ubuntu / ~3 vCPU macos-arm). pytest-xdist/pytest-split installed for experiments.
- Default suite = `pytest -m "not integration and not external"` = 5,701 tests
  (unit 4108, interfaces 1159, scripts 356; integration 77; e2e 12; external 27).
- Timings measured with no competing pytest to keep numbers clean.

## CI structure (gerrit-verify.yaml)
Critical path: clear-vote → require-ticket → build-and-test[4-cell matrix] → vote;
optionality + golden-path also gate vote. Findings:
- Test step runs SERIAL (no xdist) — dominant cost.
- NO pip caching in any workflow → heavy .[dev]→[agents] stack rebuilt every job/cell.
- Platform-independent gates (10 drift gates, make lint, pre-commit, make typecheck,
  config-check) run in ALL 4 matrix cells (test.yml factors them into ONE lint job).
- ruff 2×/cell + mypy 2×/cell (make lint + pre-commit[lint+typecheck] + make typecheck)
  → 8× each across matrix.
- require-ticket serialized in front of build-and-test (only needs to gate the vote).
- Coverage is branch=true, fail_under=70.

## Experiment battery — default suite (this Mac, py3.14; 5877 pass / 14 skip / 1 known
## Py3.14-only fail: test_resolve_symbol_confirms_third_party_module, irrelevant to CI)
| Config                                 | Wall                     | vs serial | Outcomes            |
|----------------------------------------|--------------------------|-----------|---------------------|
| serial + branch-cov (EXACT CI command) | 1361s pytest/~1700s w/rpt| 1.0×      | 5877P/14S/1F        |
| serial, no coverage                    | 1765s                    | ~1.0×     | 5877P/14S/1F        |
| -n4, no cov                            | 331s                     | 5.3×      | identical to serial |
| -n4 + branch-cov                       | 351s                     | 5.0×      | identical (cov +6%) |
| -n4 + cov + sysmon                     | 365s                     | —         | no win (IO-bound)   |
| -n auto(18), no cov                    | 218s                     | 8.1×      | identical           |
| -n auto(18) + cov + sysmon             | 254s                     | —         | rc=3 coverage SQLite race |
Peak RSS (serial) 3.28 GB.

## Slowest tests (serial+cov --durations)
- test_llm_disposition_plumbing::test_degraded_code_review_carries_disposition — 20s. Root
  cause NOT test logic (trivial asserts) — branch-coverage TRACING the first heavy
  `import rebar.llm.workflow.gate_dispatch`. Warm no-cov import of that module = 0.08s;
  coverage settrace amplifies first-heavy-import cost.
- unit/grounding/* (opengrep/ast-grep/gitleaks real-binary scans), test_code_review_* —
  5–14s: genuine security-scanner subprocess integration.
- rebar_reconciler/*acli_subprocess_timeout*, *stale_binding_404* — 9–15s: GENUINE
  subprocess-timeout/process-group reaping tests spawning real time.sleep(3600) children
  with real call_timeout+GRACE+DRAIN timers × retries. Not cheaply fakeable w/o eroding
  assurance.
- test_review_bot.py 2–6s×many (real git). e2e/test_editor_* 3–7s (Node/browser).
Conclusion: long tests are legitimate integration; lever is PARALLELISM, not per-test surgery.

## Derisking experiments for #1/#2/#6
- Integration tier xdist: full `-m integration -n4` → 1 fail = test_reconcile_live_e2e
  ::test_delete_permission_probe ("issue REB-1083 never became index-visible") — LIVE Jira
  eventual-consistency flake (marked [integration, live]); NOT an xdist defect. Excluding
  live: `-m "integration and not live and not jira_live"` → serial 64P/1S == n4 64P/1S,
  148s→94s (1.6×). Non-live integration IS xdist-safe.
- COVERAGE DEFLATION UNDER XDIST (blocker caught): naive `-n4 --cov` reported ~37% vs the
  true serial 80% and left orphaned .coverage.* files (master never combined). With
  fail_under=70 that would turn CI RED. FIX PROVEN: add `parallel = true` to
  [tool.coverage.run] → -n4 combined TOTAL exactly matches serial. One-line remedy.
- -n auto(18) unsafe: over-subscription + coverage SQLite combine race → pin -n<real cores>
  (-n4 ubuntu / -n3 macos), never `auto`.
- pip cache (#2): cold vs warm install into fresh venv w/ isolated pip cache — cold 19s →
  warm 9s (~halves install; cache ~52 MB; heavy stack genuinely pulled: anthropic,
  opentelemetry, pydantic-ai confirmed). Modest absolute win; corrected down from earlier
  "2–4 min" guess.
- --durations (#6): `--durations=25` works with -q + cov + xdist; pytest-split
  `--store-durations --durations-path .test_durations` writes valid JSON (41 entries subset).
- Prereq: pytest-xdist + pytest-split ABSENT from [dev] — must be added pinned.

## Test-isolation verification (explicit user constraint)
-n4 produced byte-identical outcomes to serial (same 5877P/14S + same 1 fail) with ZERO
spurious failures from autouse isolation fixtures (per-test tmp_path, XDG_CONFIG_HOME,
monkeypatch, config-cache reset, socket guard, repo-leak guard, session repo-HEAD backstop).
Only instability was -n auto (coverage SQLite, not test cross-talk). Isolation holds at -n4.

## OSS research (subagent; authorized)
Closest analog: Trail of Bits made PyPI/Warehouse ~4,700-test suite 163s→30s (81%) via
xdist + COVERAGE_CORE=sysmon + testpaths. Transferable:
- `-n auto` over-subscribes on CI → pin to real cores. Parallelism exposes hidden coupling.
- sysmon ~53% off coverage but LINE-only; branch+sysmon needs 3.14 — on 3.11–3.13 with
  branch=true it SILENTLY no-ops. (Irrelevant here: coverage overhead ~6%, suite IO-bound.)
- pytest-cov × xdist needs parallel + combine or numbers deflate (we hit exactly this).
- pytest-split shards across runners (durations-cache race → use artifacts).
- testmon/impact-analysis moves assurance in time; fine pre-gate, NOT the authoritative gate
  (misses subprocess/data-file deps — this suite has many).
- Tiered matrix (reduced pre-merge + full post-merge/nightly); fail-fast:false on gate;
  de-dup mypy/ruff (run once, not per cell).

## Proven conclusions
1. xdist -n<real cores> is THE lever: ~5× at -n4, isolation-safe, assurance-neutral.
2. Coverage overhead ~6% → KEEP coverage on gate.
3. Use -n4/-n3, not -n auto.
4. Coverage under xdist REQUIRES parallel=true (proven fix).
5. Don't chase sysmon (no win; version-gated on CI).
6. pip cache real but modest (~halves install).
7. Slow tests are legitimate integration → parallelism, not surgery.

## Proposed plan (ranked; on 7654) — projected ~32–42 min → ~10–12 min
#1 xdist -n<real cores> --dist worksteal (+parallel=true fix; non-live integration; live
tests serial/xdist_group). #2 pip caching on all setup-python. #3 factor platform-independent
gates into one job. #4 de-dup mypy/ruff. #5 take require-ticket off critical path. #6 bake
--durations=25 into gate. Do-NOT: drop coverage, use -n auto, sysmon, or testmon-on-gate.
Optional: tiered matrix (only real assurance trade-off).

## Epic authored & plan-reviewed
Epic ce0e scopes #1/#2/#6 (#3/#4/#5/tiered-matrix explicitly OUT → follow-up epic). Children
8d36 (xdist + parallel=true + docs/coverage.md), bad4 (pip cache), 9ad9 (--durations); all
pass check_ac. Plan review PASS, certified; one advisory (parent AC-3 docs update unowned)
remediated by assigning docs/coverage.md to 8d36, re-reviewed → PASS. Not claimed/implemented.

Raw logs: $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/perf (baseline_cov.log, run_*.log, summary.txt,
install_summary.txt, battery.sh, install_spike.sh).
